quizzes.name: Animal Cell – Structure & Functions
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of animal cell organelles and their roles.
quizzes.subject: Biology

questions.text: Which organelle is known as the 'powerhouse of the cell'?
What is the function of the nucleus in an animal cell?

answers.text: Controls cell activities and contains DNA

questions.text: Which organelle is responsible for protein synthesis?
Animal cells do NOT have a cell wall.
The Golgi apparatus is involved in which of the following?
Which of the following is present in animal cells but NOT in plant cells?
Lysosomes contain digestive enzymes that break down waste materials.
What is the outer boundary of an animal cell called?
The endoplasmic reticulum can be smooth or rough. What makes rough ER 'rough'?
Which structure controls what enters and exits the nucleus?

quizzes.name: Photosynthesis & Cellular Respiration
quizzes.description: Explore the two fundamental energy processes in living organisms.
quizzes.subject: Biology

questions.text: What is the overall equation for photosynthesis?
In which organelle does photosynthesis take place?
Cellular respiration produces ATP.
Which pigment in chloroplasts absorbs light for photosynthesis?
Aerobic respiration requires which gas?
The light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis occur in which part of the chloroplast?
How many ATP molecules are produced per glucose in aerobic respiration (approximate)?
Anaerobic respiration in yeast produces ethanol and carbon dioxide.

quizzes.name: Periodic Table – Elements
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of element symbols, groups, and properties.
quizzes.subject: Chemistry

questions.text: What is the chemical symbol for Gold?
How many elements are in the modern periodic table?
What is the atomic number of Carbon?
Noble gases are found in Group 18 of the periodic table.
What is the symbol for Sodium?
Which element has the highest electronegativity?
Isotopes of the same element have the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons.
Which group in the periodic table contains Alkali metals?

quizzes.name: Acids, Bases & pH Scale
quizzes.description: Understand acids, bases, neutralisation and the pH scale.
quizzes.subject: Chemistry

questions.text: What pH value indicates a neutral solution?
Which ion makes a solution acidic?
A pH of 3 is more acidic than a pH of 5.
Which of the following is a strong acid?
When an acid and a base react together, the reaction is called?
Litmus paper turns red in acidic conditions and blue in alkaline conditions.

quizzes.name: Newton's Laws of Motion
quizzes.description: Understand the three fundamental laws that describe motion.
quizzes.subject: Physics

questions.text: Newton's First Law states that an object at rest stays at rest unless acted on by an unbalanced force.
What does Newton's Second Law express? (F = ?)

answers.text: F = mv (force equals mass times velocity)
F = mg (force equals mass times gravity only)
F = ma (force equals mass times acceleration)

questions.text: Newton's Third Law states that for every action there is an equal and opposite reaction.
What is the SI unit of force?
An object with greater mass requires more force to achieve the same acceleration as a lighter object.
What property of matter resists changes in motion?

quizzes.name: Electricity & Circuits
quizzes.description: Explore voltage, current, resistance, and circuit types.
quizzes.subject: Physics

questions.text: What does Ohm's Law state?
What is the SI unit of electrical resistance?
In a series circuit, the current is the same through all components.
In a parallel circuit, what happens to the total resistance as more branches are added?
Electric current is measured in which unit?

quizzes.name: Functions & Limits – Baccalaureate
quizzes.description: Prepare for the baccalaureate with functions, limits and continuity.
quizzes.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: What is the limit of (sin x)/x as x approaches 0?
If f(x) = x², what is f'(x)?
A function is continuous at x = a if the limit exists and equals f(a).
What is the domain of f(x) = 1/x?
What is the integral of 2x with respect to x?

quizzes.name: Quadratic Equations & Inequalities
quizzes.description: Solve quadratic equations using multiple methods.
quizzes.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: What are the solutions of x² - 5x + 6 = 0?
The discriminant of ax² + bx + c = 0 is given by Δ = b² - 4ac.
If the discriminant Δ < 0, the quadratic has:
Solve: x² = 16. What are the values of x?

quizzes.subject: Geography

questions.text: What is the capital of Australia?
What is the capital of Brazil?
Tokyo is the capital of Japan.
What is the capital of Canada?
What is the capital of South Africa? (Legislative)

Nairobi is the capital of Kenya.

questions.text: Care este capitala României?

quizzes.name: World War II – Key Events
quizzes.description: Major battles, dates and turning points of WWII.
quizzes.subject: History

questions.text: In which year did World War II begin?
Which event brought the United States into WWII?
Operation Overlord was the Allied invasion of Normandy in June 1944.
Germany surrendered in which month and year?
Who was the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom for most of WWII?
The atomic bombs were dropped on Hiroshima and Nagasaki in 1945.
The Battle of Stalingrad was a turning point on which front?

quizzes.name: Ancient Rome – Republic & Empire
quizzes.description: From SPQR to the fall of Rome.
quizzes.subject: History

questions.text: According to tradition, in what year was Rome founded?
Julius Caesar was the first Emperor of Rome.
Who was the first Emperor of Rome?
SPQR stands for Senatus Populusque Romanus (The Senate and People of Rome).
The Western Roman Empire fell in which year?

quizzes.name: Sorting Algorithms
quizzes.description: Understand the most common sorting algorithms and their complexity.
quizzes.subject: Computer Science

questions.text: What is the best-case time complexity of Bubble Sort?
Merge Sort has a time complexity of O(n log n) in all cases.
Which algorithm uses a 'divide and conquer' approach?
What is the worst-case complexity of Quick Sort?
Insertion Sort is efficient for small or nearly-sorted datasets.

quizzes.name: Object-Oriented Programming Concepts
quizzes.description: Core OOP principles: encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, abstraction.
quizzes.subject: Computer Science

questions.text: Which OOP principle hides internal implementation details from the outside world?
Inheritance allows a child class to acquire the properties of a parent class.
What is method overloading?

answers.text: Same method name and parameters in a subclass
Hiding a method with private access

questions.text: What is an abstract class?

answers.text: A class that inherits from multiple classes
A class that cannot be instantiated and may contain abstract methods
A class with only static methods

questions.text: Polymorphism allows the same method name to behave differently in different classes.

quizzes.name: Romanian Grammar – Parts of Speech
quizzes.description: Identificați și înțelegeți părțile de vorbire în limba română.
quizzes.subject: Language & Literature

questions.text: Care este funcția sintactică a cuvântului subliniat: «Ea cântă frumos»?
Substantivul este o parte de vorbire flexibilă.
Care dintre următoarele cuvinte este un pronume?
Verbul 'a fi' poate fi copulativ sau auxiliar.
Care este forma de plural a cuvântului «carte»?

quizzes.name: English Grammar – Tenses
quizzes.description: Master English verb tenses from simple to perfect continuous.
quizzes.subject: Language & Literature

questions.text: Which sentence uses the Present Perfect tense?
The Past Continuous tense uses 'was/were + verb-ing'.
Which tense describes a completed action at a specific time in the past?
Fill in: 'By the time he arrived, she _______ (already leave).'

quizzes.name: Cognitive Psychology Basics
quizzes.description: Explore memory, attention, perception and cognitive processes.
quizzes.subject: Psychology

questions.text: Which type of memory has an almost unlimited capacity and stores information indefinitely?
The 'magic number' proposed by Miller for short-term memory capacity is 7 ± 2 chunks.
What is the term for interpreting sensory information to understand our environment?
Selective attention allows us to focus on one stimulus while filtering out others.
Which cognitive bias leads people to favour information confirming their existing beliefs?

quizzes.name: Freud's Psychoanalytic Theory
quizzes.description: Id, Ego, Superego, defence mechanisms and the unconscious mind.
quizzes.subject: Psychology

questions.text: According to Freud, which part of the psyche operates on the 'pleasure principle'?
The Ego operates on the 'reality principle'.
Which defence mechanism involves pushing uncomfortable thoughts into the unconscious?
Freud proposed five stages of psychosexual development.
In Freud's psychosexual stages, which stage occurs roughly between ages 6–12?
